title: Windle Trust International Postgraduate Scholarship Programme 2026 in the United Kingdom for Conflict-Affected Students from Kenya and Uganda
summary: For many talented students living in conflict-affected communities, access to quality postgraduate education remains one of the biggest barriers to career advancement and community transformation. The Windle Trust International Postgraduate Scholarship Programme 2026 is creating a life-changing opportunity for refugees and conflict-affected individuals residing in Kenya and Uganda to pursue fully funded Master’s degree studies in the United Kingdom. This prestigious scholarship programme, funded primarily through the Commonwealth Scholarships Commission Master’s and Shared Scholarships schemes, is designed to empower future leaders, professionals, educators, researchers, and changemakers who are committed to driving development in their countries and communities. Applications are officially open from 15 May to 12 June 2026 . About the Windle Trust International Postgraduate Scholarship Programme Windle Trust International (WTI) serves as a nominating partner for the Commonwealth Scholarships Commission (CSC). Through this partnership, eligible candidates receive extensive support throughout the scholarship and university application process. Applicants who successfully pass the WTI selection process will: Receive professional guidance on applying to UK universities Obtain support in preparing Commonwealth Scholarship Commission applications Be considered for nomination by Windle Trust International to the CSC Gain access to tailored scholarship application guidance and support materials The programme aims to support individuals whose education and career opportunities have been disrupted by conflict and displacement while helping them gain advanced knowledge that contributes to sustainable development in their communities. Scholarship Benefits Successful applicants will receive a fully funded Master’s scholarship package that includes: Full Tuition Fees The scholarship covers the full cost of tuition at participating UK universities. Monthly Maintenance Stipend Selected scholars will receive financial support to cover living expenses while studying in the United Kingdom. Relocation Costs The programme provides relocation assistance to help scholars transition smoothly to the UK for their studies. University Application Support WTI provides guidance and assistance throughout the university application process. Commonwealth Scholarship Commission Application Support Applicants receive tailored support for completing the CSC scholarship application successfully. Access to Exclusive Scholarship Guidance Materials Shortlisted candidates gain access to: Question-by-question guidance Scholarship application examples General scholarship writing tips The complete Windle-CSC Application Guide Eligible Fields of Study One of the strongest features of this scholarship programme is its flexibility. All subject areas are eligible; however, priority is often given to courses that contribute directly to national and community development. Priority fields include: Education Social Work Development Studies Public Health Nutrition Crop Science Animal Science Gender Studies Renewable Energy Climate Change Development-related Law Agroforestry and Food Security English Language Education Computer Science Engineering These areas are particularly encouraged because of their potential impact on economic growth, sustainability, healthcare improvement, food security, technological advancement, and social development. Where Can Scholars Study? Applicants may apply to universities in the United Kingdom that participate in the Commonwealth Scholarships Commission schemes. WTI strongly encourages applicants to target universities that do not charge application fees. While Windle Trust International has negotiated fee waivers with some universities, fee waivers are not available universally. Importantly: WTI does not pay university application fees Applicants should confirm fee waiver availability before applying Enquiries regarding fee waivers can be sent to postgraduate@windle.org.uk Eligibility Requirements To qualify for the Windle Trust International Postgraduate Scholarship Programme 2026, applicants must meet all eligibility criteria without exception. Applicants must: Have refugee status OR reside in specific conflict-affected areas in Kenya or Uganda Demonstrate excellent English language proficiency Hold a minimum academic qualification equivalent to a British Upper Second-Class (2:1) undergraduate degree Possess at least two years of relevant professional or volunteer experience Applicants are advised to read the complete Eligibility Guidance Document carefully before submitting an application because eligibility requirements are strictly enforced. Application Process Interested candidates must submit applications through the Windle Trust International online application portal. Application Timeline Opening Date: 15 May 2026 Deadline: 12 June 2026 Late applications may not be considered, so applicants are encouraged to begin preparing early. Selection Process The selection process involves multiple stages designed to identify highly motivated and qualified candidates. Step 1: Online Application Applicants submit their applications through the WTI online portal. Step 2: Shortlisting Eligible candidates are shortlisted based on academic merit, experience, leadership potential, and alignment with development goals. Step 3: Interview Shortlisted applicants are invited for interviews either: Online, or In their country of residence Step 4: Scholarship and University Application Support Candidates who pass the interview stage receive: Tailored CSC application support UK university application assistance Guidance materials and mentoring Step 5: Final Scholarship Decision Final scholarship awards are determined by the Commonwealth Scholarships Commission. Commitment to Inclusion and Equal Opportunity Windle Trust International promotes equal opportunity and non-discrimination throughout the selection process. The organization strongly encourages applications from: Women Refugees Minority groups Persons living with disabilities Individuals affected by conflict This inclusive approach ensures that deserving candidates from vulnerable and underrepresented backgrounds have access to quality higher education opportunities. Why This Scholarship Matters The Windle Trust International Postgraduate Scholarship Programme is more than a scholarship. It is an investment in future leaders who can contribute meaningfully to rebuilding communities affected by conflict and displacement. By supporting postgraduate education in critical development sectors, the programme empowers scholars to: Improve healthcare systems Strengthen educational institutions Advance climate resilience Promote sustainable agriculture Enhance technological innovation Advocate for social justice and inclusion Graduates are expected to apply their knowledge and skills toward solving pressing challenges in their communities and countries. Important Notes for Applicants Before applying, candidates should keep the following in mind: WTI never charges fees for scholarship applications Eligibility requirements are applied strictly University application fees are not covered by WTI Applicants should prepare all documents early Strong academic and professional records improve competitiveness For application guidance and enquiries, applicants may contact: Email: postgraduate@windle.org.uk APPLY NOW KENYA APPLY NOW UGANDA Final Thoughts The Windle Trust International Postgraduate Scholarship Programme 2026 represents a remarkable opportunity for conflict-affected individuals in Kenya and Uganda to pursue fully funded Master’s studies in the United Kingdom. Beyond financial support, the programme provides mentorship, application guidance, and academic opportunities that can transform careers and communities. For refugees and conflict-affected individuals seeking to become leaders in development, education, healthcare, technology, agriculture, and sustainability, this scholarship could be the gateway to a brighter future. Applicants are encouraged to prepare thoroughly and submit their applications before the 12 June 2026 deadline . For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Scholarship Opportunities on OFY here . The post Windle Trust International Postgraduate Scholarship Programme 2026 in the United Kingdom for Conflict-Affected Students from Kenya and Uganda appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: AmaliTech Rwanda Paid Apprenticeship Programmes 2026 for Software Development and Data Engineering Professionals
summary: The AmaliTech Apprenticeship Programmes 2026 in Kigali, Rwanda, present an exceptional opportunity for aspiring and experienced technology professionals seeking to build globally competitive careers in software development, data engineering, quality assurance, and DevOps. Through its structured apprenticeship tracks, AmaliTech is equipping African tech talent with practical experience, international-standard training, and direct exposure to real-world client projects. The programmes are designed for two categories of professionals: Early-career talents and recent graduates seeking intensive mentorship and career acceleration. Mid-level professionals with prior industry experience looking to deepen their expertise and transition into globally competitive engineering roles. Both apprenticeship tracks are fully paid and based on-site in Kigali, Rwanda, providing participants with immersive learning experiences under the guidance of senior engineers. About the AmaliTech Apprenticeship Programmes AmaliTech is known for building high-quality digital and technology talent across Africa while connecting professionals to global opportunities. Its apprenticeship programmes combine technical training, project-based learning, mentorship, and exposure to international delivery standards. Participants in the programme work on real-world projects and gain practical skills aligned with global industry expectations. Apprentices are trained in collaborative software engineering environments while sharpening both technical and professional competencies. The apprenticeships are conducted full-time on-site from Monday to Friday in Kigali, Rwanda. Available Apprenticeship Tracks Participants can specialize in several high-demand technology areas, including: Java Backend Development Python Backend and App Development JavaScript Full Stack Development Data Engineering Quality Assurance (QA) DevOps Engineering These specializations are designed to prepare participants for modern technology careers and equip them with industry-relevant expertise. Early-Career Apprenticeship Programme The first apprenticeship programme is a six-month full-time paid opportunity specifically tailored for: Final-year university students approaching graduation Recent graduates Early-career technology professionals The programme focuses on helping emerging tech talent transition into professional engineering careers through hands-on experience and mentorship. What Participants Will Gain Participants in the early-career programme will benefit from: Intensive mentorship from senior engineers Practical project-based learning Exposure to international client projects Industry-standard engineering practices Collaborative team environments Career acceleration opportunities Technical and professional skill development Real-world software engineering experience Eligibility Requirements for Early-Career Applicants To qualify for the programme, applicants must meet the following requirements: Educational Qualifications Applicants should be: Final-year university students approaching graduation, or Recent graduates in: Computer Science Information Technology Engineering Data Science Related fields Technical Experience Applicants must possess at least six months of practical programming experience obtained through: Internship programmes Training programmes School projects Personal projects Hackathons Online courses Freelance or self-learning experiences Additional Requirements Candidates must also demonstrate: Passion for software development Strong interest in long-term technology careers Ability to work independently Initiative and accountability Growth-oriented mindset Adaptability and willingness to learn Strong teamwork skills Excellent problem-solving abilities Effective communication skills Fluency in spoken and written English Applicants must also be: Currently based in Rwanda, or Willing to relocate to Kigali, Rwanda Mid-Level Professional Apprenticeship Programme AmaliTech also offers a second apprenticeship track designed specifically for mid-level professionals. This four-month full-time paid apprenticeship is intended for professionals who already possess industry experience and want to advance into more specialized technical roles. The programme focuses on advanced upskilling and practical exposure to industry-driven projects. Focus Areas for Mid-Level Professionals Participants in this programme will deepen their expertise in: Java Backend Development Python Backend and App Development JavaScript Full Stack Development Data Engineering Quality Assurance (QA) DevOps Engineering The apprenticeship emphasizes advanced technical growth and direct project delivery experience within professional engineering environments. Benefits of the AmaliTech Apprenticeship Programmes Participants selected for either programme will enjoy numerous professional and career benefits, including: 1. Fully Paid Apprenticeship Both programmes are paid opportunities, allowing participants to learn and grow professionally while receiving financial support. 2. Real-World Industry Experience Apprentices gain direct experience working on live projects that support international clients. 3. Mentorship from Senior Engineers Participants receive guidance from experienced professionals who help shape their technical and professional development. 4. Career Advancement Opportunities The programmes are designed to prepare apprentices for long-term careers in global technology industries. 5. International Engineering Standards Participants are trained according to internationally recognized delivery and engineering standards. 6. Opportunity for Full-Time Employment One of the most attractive features of the programme is the possibility of securing a full-time role at AmaliTech after successful completion. What Happens After the Apprenticeship? At the end of the apprenticeship period, participants undergo a structured assessment process. The evaluation is based on several factors, including: Technical competence Professionalism Delivery reliability Collaboration skills Readiness for independent project execution Alignment with international engineering standards Participants who successfully meet the required performance expectations and demonstrate strong professional readiness may receive full-time job offers within AmaliTech. This provides a direct pathway into long-term employment and career growth within an internationally connected technology company. Why the AmaliTech Apprenticeship Stands Out The AmaliTech Apprenticeship Programme is not just another training initiative. It serves as a launchpad for emerging and growing technology professionals across Africa. What makes the programme unique includes: Strong focus on practical learning Real client project exposure Career-focused mentorship Access to international engineering practices Direct pathway to employment Highly in-demand technical specializations Structured professional development environment For aspiring software engineers, backend developers, DevOps professionals, QA engineers, and data engineers, this programme offers a rare opportunity to gain globally relevant experience while building a sustainable career in technology. Application Status Applications are currently open for both apprenticeship programmes. Deadline No application deadline has been specified yet. Interested candidates are encouraged to apply as early as possible before slots are filled. APPLY HERE Applicants whose preferred specialization may not currently be available are still encouraged to submit their CVs for consideration in future cohorts. For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Internship Opportunities on OFY here . title: Fully Funded UK PhD 2026 at Lancaster University on Healthcare Carbon Emissions, Virtual Wards and Patient Care Decisions
summary: Applications are now open for a fully funded PhD opportunity at Lancaster University focused on healthcare sustainability, carbon emissions, patient care decision-making, and virtual ward systems within acute healthcare settings in England. This exciting interdisciplinary doctoral research project is designed for students passionate about health inequalities, public health, environmental sustainability, healthcare systems, sociology, psychology, nursing, and implementation science. The PhD project, titled “Exploring Patient and Carer Understandings of and Perspectives on Carbon Emissions When Making Virtual Ward Care Decisions Within Acute Care Settings,” offers successful applicants a fully funded studentship including an annual stipend of £21,805 per year alongside tuition fee coverage for eligible home students. The research is highly relevant to the future of healthcare systems globally, especially as healthcare institutions increasingly focus on sustainability and reducing carbon emissions while maintaining quality patient care. About the Research Project The National Health Service (NHS) in England became the first healthcare system globally to commit to ambitious net-zero carbon emissions targets: Net zero for direct emissions by 2040 Net zero for broader emissions by 2045 Healthcare delivery contributes significantly to carbon emissions within the NHS. According to the project information: Care provision accounts for approximately 24% of NHS carbon emissions. Acute hospital care contributes around 56% of emissions related to care delivery. Acute care represents approximately 13% of all NHS England emissions. This makes acute healthcare settings one of the largest contributors to healthcare-related environmental impact in England. The PhD project seeks to understand how patients and carers perceive the environmental impact of healthcare and how carbon emission information may influence healthcare decisions, especially regarding virtual ward care systems. Focus Areas of the PhD Research The successful PhD candidate will investigate patient and carer perspectives surrounding environmentally sustainable healthcare and virtual care delivery models. Research Areas Include: Understanding patient and carer awareness of healthcare-related carbon emissions Exploring how environmental information may influence healthcare choices Investigating virtual ward systems in acute care settings Studying experiences related to: Heart failure Respiratory illnesses Frailty-related conditions Co-designing patient guidance on environmentally sustainable healthcare Evaluating implementation strategies and impact What the Successful Candidate Will Do The selected doctoral researcher will undertake several important research and engagement activities throughout the PhD programme. Key Responsibilities Include: 1. Conduct a Systematic Review The student will carry out a comprehensive literature review focused on: Patient and carer understanding of healthcare environmental impacts Carbon emissions associated with healthcare delivery Sustainable healthcare communication 2. Deliver Interviews and Workshops The project involves: Conducting patient and carer interviews Organizing deliberative workshops Analysing qualitative data Gathering experiences from individuals affected by heart failure, respiratory illness, and frailty-related conditions 3. Facilitate Co-Design Activities The candidate will work collaboratively with patients and carers to develop guidance materials related to: Environmental impacts of acute healthcare Sustainable healthcare decision-making Patient-centered communication tools The project will also use theoretical and conceptual models linked to co-design methodologies. 4. Evaluate Implementation and Impact The student will: Design and deliver implementation evaluations Explore real-world application of the developed toolkit Use implementation science approaches to assess outcomes and impact 5. Collaborate with Research Teams and PPIE Groups There will also be opportunities to: Work alongside interdisciplinary research teams Collaborate with Patient Public Involvement and Engagement (PPIE) groups Support public engagement strategies Contribute to evaluation activities Funding and Financial Benefits This PhD studentship offers substantial financial support for eligible candidates. Funding Includes: Fully funded PhD studentship Annual stipend of £21,805 Tuition fee coverage for home students Opportunity to engage in impactful healthcare research Access to interdisciplinary academic supervision and networks The funding duration aligns with standard UK doctoral programme structures. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the academic qualifications required for admission. Applicants Should Have: Undergraduate Degree A strong first degree (minimum 2:1 classification or equivalent) in a relevant discipline such as: Nursing Psychology Sociology Public health Medical humanities Social sciences Health sciences Master’s Degree A Master’s degree with at least Merit level (or equivalent) in a relevant social science or healthcare-related discipline is preferred. Important Dates Application Deadline 15 June 2026 Interview Period Week commencing 29 June 2026 Programme Start Date 28 September 2026 Applicants are encouraged to prepare their applications early to avoid missing the deadline. Apply Here Supervisors and Contact Information Prospective applicants may contact the project supervisors for further discussion or clarification regarding the research proposal and application process. Supervisors Professor Heather Brown Professor Amanda Taylor-Beswick Why This PhD Opportunity Stands Out This PhD offers a unique opportunity to work at the intersection of: Healthcare sustainability Environmental policy Patient-centred care Public health Health inequalities Implementation science Virtual healthcare systems The project is especially relevant in today’s global healthcare environment where sustainability and carbon reduction strategies are becoming increasingly important. Students interested in climate-conscious healthcare systems and socially impactful research will find this doctoral opportunity highly valuable. Career Opportunities After the PhD Graduates from this programme may pursue careers in: Health policy research Public health institutions NHS research and innovation Academia and teaching Healthcare sustainability consulting Health economics Implementation science Environmental health policy Patient engagement and healthcare innovation Final Thoughts As healthcare systems worldwide seek sustainable and environmentally responsible models of care delivery, research opportunities like this fully funded Lancaster University PhD are becoming increasingly significant. This programme not only provides strong financial support but also allows students to contribute to globally relevant healthcare transformation and sustainability goals. title: United Nations OCHA Associate Communications Officer / Content Creator (P-2) Job in The Hague, Netherlands
summary: The United Nations Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs (OCHA) has officially opened applications for the position of Associate Communications Officer / Content Creator (P-2) based in The Hague, Netherlands. This is an outstanding international career opportunity for communication professionals who are passionate about humanitarian action, digital storytelling, advocacy, public engagement, and global impact. The position is part of the Partnerships Communications Unit within the Public Engagement Section of OCHA’s Communications Branch. Successful candidates will support communication strategies aimed at strengthening advocacy, visibility, fundraising efforts, and public engagement for humanitarian causes worldwide. This United Nations career opportunity offers professionals the chance to contribute directly to humanitarian coordination and emergency response communication efforts while working in a globally respected international organization. About the United Nations OCHA The United Nations Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs , commonly known as OCHA, is part of the United Nations Secretariat responsible for coordinating humanitarian responses during emergencies and disasters. OCHA works to: Bring humanitarian actors together Ensure coordinated emergency responses Advocate for people affected by crises Promote preparedness and prevention Support sustainable humanitarian solutions Mobilize humanitarian assistance globally The organization plays a critical role in ensuring that aid reaches vulnerable populations efficiently and effectively during humanitarian emergencies. Overview of the Associate Communications Officer / Content Creator Position The Associate Communications Officer / Content Creator role is designed for professionals with expertise in communication strategy, digital engagement, advocacy campaigns, storytelling, content creation, and public outreach. The selected candidate will support OCHA’s communication objectives by helping design and implement impactful communication campaigns that increase visibility and strengthen engagement with stakeholders and audiences worldwide. Job Details Position Title: Associate Communications Officer / Content Creator Job Opening ID: 276433 Job Network: Public Information and Conference Management Job Family: Public Information Category and Level: Professional and Higher Categories, P-2 Duty Station: The Hague, Netherlands Department/Office: Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs (OCHA) Date Posted: 29 April 2026 Application Deadline: 28 May 2026 Key Responsibilities of the Position The Associate Communications Officer / Content Creator will undertake a broad range of communication and advocacy-related responsibilities. These responsibilities may include: 1. Communication Strategy Support The selected candidate will: Assist in developing communication strategies Support advocacy and visibility campaigns Promote humanitarian messaging effectively Help strengthen fundraising communication efforts 2. Digital Content Creation Responsibilities may include: Producing multimedia and digital content Developing engaging storytelling materials Supporting social media communication Creating audience-centered public information materials 3. Campaign Monitoring and Analytics The role may involve: Monitoring communication campaigns Evaluating digital engagement metrics Tracking communication performance Analyzing outreach effectiveness 4. Public Engagement and Advocacy The candidate will support: Advocacy initiatives Outreach campaigns Internal communication activities Stakeholder engagement efforts 5. Humanitarian Communication Support The position contributes to: Crisis communication Public awareness initiatives Humanitarian visibility campaigns Emergency communication coordination Why This UN Job Opportunity Is Important Working with OCHA provides professionals with the opportunity to directly contribute to global humanitarian efforts and international development initiatives. This role is particularly valuable for communication professionals seeking: International work experience Exposure to humanitarian operations Career growth within the United Nations system Opportunities to influence global audiences Experience in strategic communication and advocacy Professionals in this position will work in a multicultural environment alongside international experts committed to humanitarian action and sustainable global solutions. Benefits of Working with the United Nations Although the full compensation package is not detailed in the announcement, UN Professional Category positions generally offer several benefits, including: Competitive international salary Health insurance and benefits International career development opportunities Multicultural work environment Professional networking opportunities Potential mobility within the UN system Meaningful global impact through humanitarian work Work Location: The Hague, Netherlands The selected candidate will be based in The Hague, one of the world’s leading international cities and home to numerous global organizations, international courts, and diplomatic institutions. Working in The Hague offers: An international professional environment Access to global policy and humanitarian networks Exposure to international governance systems High-quality professional development opportunities Who Should Apply? This role is ideal for: Communication professionals Journalists and media practitioners Public relations specialists Digital storytellers Social media strategists Advocacy and outreach professionals Humanitarian communication experts International affairs communicators Candidates passionate about humanitarian action, public engagement, and impactful storytelling are strongly encouraged to apply. Skills and Qualities Needed Successful applicants are likely to possess: Strong written and verbal communication skills Digital communication expertise Content creation and storytelling abilities Analytical and campaign monitoring skills Creativity and innovation Ability to work in multicultural environments Interest in humanitarian affairs and international cooperation Application Deadline Deadline: 28 May 2026 Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications early to avoid last-minute technical issues and to ensure all required documentation is properly completed. How to Apply Interested applicants should: Visit the official United Nations Careers portal Search for Job Opening ID: 276433 Complete the online application Upload required documents and credentials Submit the application before the deadline Applicants should carefully review: Education requirements Work experience criteria Language requirements Competency expectations before submitting their applications. Apply HERE Why You Should Consider This Opportunity The United Nations OCHA Associate Communications Officer / Content Creator position is more than just a communications job. It is an opportunity to become part of a global mission focused on saving lives, supporting vulnerable communities, and strengthening humanitarian responses worldwide. For professionals interested in combining communication expertise with meaningful international impact, this role offers a rare chance to build a globally recognized career while contributing to humanitarian action on an international scale. title: UNESCO ICT in Education Prize 2026: Win US$25,000 for Innovative AI Projects Transforming Creativity and Critical Thinking in Education
summary: Artificial Intelligence is rapidly transforming the future of education, reshaping the way learners think, create, solve problems, and interact with knowledge. As AI tools become increasingly powerful in generating content, solving complex problems, and automating intellectual tasks, educators worldwide are facing an urgent question: How can education preserve and strengthen human creativity, imagination, and critical thinking in the age of AI? To address this important challenge, the UNESCO has officially opened nominations for the 2026 edition of the UNESCO King Hamad Bin Isa Al-Khalifa Prize for the Use of ICT in Education. The prestigious international award seeks to recognize outstanding projects that demonstrate how artificial intelligence can enhance—not replace—human thinking and creativity in learning environments. The 2026 edition is themed: “Reimagining Creativity and Critical Thinking with Artificial Intelligence” This global opportunity aims to spotlight innovative educational projects that use AI responsibly while empowering learners and teachers to strengthen imagination, reasoning, discernment, creativity, and problem-solving skills. The prize offers international recognition, a diploma, and a financial reward of US$25,000 for each of the two selected winners. About the UNESCO ICT in Education Prize The UNESCO ICT in Education Prize was established to celebrate groundbreaking initiatives that effectively use information and communication technologies (ICT) to improve education systems and learning outcomes globally. For the 2026 edition, UNESCO is focusing specifically on how AI can be integrated into education without diminishing the human capacity for critical thought and creativity. According to UNESCO, education has a central role to play in ensuring that AI serves as a tool for expanding human agency rather than becoming a substitute for independent thinking. While AI systems can now compose essays, generate ideas, solve equations, and produce information instantly, there is growing concern that learners may become overly dependent on these technologies. The prize therefore seeks projects that encourage learners to: Think independently Explore creatively Question information critically Solve problems innovatively Engage thoughtfully with AI systems Preserve imagination and originality Why This UNESCO Prize Matters Artificial intelligence is becoming deeply integrated into classrooms, universities, online learning platforms, and informal education spaces worldwide. However, many educators and policymakers are concerned about the “flattening effect” AI could have on human creativity and intellectual development if used improperly. This UNESCO initiative highlights the importance of balancing technological advancement with human-centered learning. Winning projects are expected to demonstrate how AI can: Support creative learning experiences Encourage analytical reasoning Strengthen imagination and curiosity Promote ethical and thoughtful AI use Complement human intelligence rather than replace it The award also provides a platform for global visibility, helping successful initiatives inspire educators and institutions around the world. Prize Benefits Selected winners of the UNESCO ICT in Education Prize 2026 will receive: 1. Financial Reward Each of the two winners will receive: US$25,000 2. International Recognition Winning projects will gain: Global exposure through UNESCO platforms International credibility and prestige Recognition from leading education and AI experts 3. Official Diploma Awardees will receive an official UNESCO diploma recognizing their achievement and contribution to education innovation. 4. Award Ceremony Participation Winners will be recognized during an international award ceremony scheduled for September 2026. Theme Focus: Reimagining Creativity and Critical Thinking with AI The 2026 edition specifically seeks projects that demonstrate meaningful and innovative engagement with AI systems in educational settings. UNESCO is particularly interested in initiatives showing how: Teachers use AI to improve creativity-based learning Students use AI responsibly to enhance learning outcomes AI tools support deeper thinking and inquiry Educational systems preserve human reasoning and imagination Learning environments encourage originality despite AI automation Projects can come from: Formal education settings Non-formal learning spaces Informal education environments Community learning initiatives Digital education ecosystems Who Can Apply? Applications are open to a broad range of participants, including: Individuals Schools and universities NGOs Research institutions Educational organizations Innovation hubs Other entities working in education and AI However, applicants must meet the following condition: Project Requirement The submitted project must: Already be operational Have existed for at least one year Align with the 2026 theme on creativity, critical thinking, and AI Important Nomination Requirement One of the most important aspects of the application process is that self-nominations are not allowed. Applications must be submitted through: A UNESCO National Commission from a Member State, or An NGO officially partnered with UNESCO UNESCO encourages applicants to contact the relevant National Commission or partner NGO early in the application process. Additionally: Governments of UNESCO Member States NGOs in official partnership with UNESCO may nominate up to three projects. Selection Process An international jury will review all eligible nominations and evaluate projects based on: Innovation Educational impact Relevance to the 2026 theme Sustainability Effectiveness in promoting creativity and critical thinking through AI The final winners will be selected by the Director-General of UNESCO following the jury’s recommendations. Deadline for Applications Nomination Deadline: 29 May 2026 (23:59 CEST) Applicants are strongly encouraged to begin the nomination process early because coordination with National Commissions or UNESCO partner NGOs may require additional time. How to Apply Interested applicants should: Prepare documentation about their project Ensure the project aligns with the 2026 theme Contact a UNESCO National Commission or official UNESCO partner NGO Request nomination support Submit the application through the authorized nominating body Official Links and Contact Information For full application details and nomination guidelines, visit the official UNESCO Prize webpage and UNESCO website. For inquiries: Email: ictprize@unesco.org This opportunity represents a major global platform for innovators shaping the future of AI in education responsibly and creatively. Whether you are an educator, institution, or organization developing transformative AI learning experiences, the UNESCO ICT in Education Prize 2026 offers a unique opportunity to gain international recognition and contribute to the future of human-centered education. title: AfricaNenda Foundation Paid Program Support Internship 2026: Remote Opportunity for Young Professionals Across Africa
summary: AfricaNenda Foundation has announced an exciting paid internship opportunity for young professionals interested in contributing to the growth of inclusive instant payment systems across Africa. The organization is currently recruiting a Program Support Intern to join its Projects Team in a fully remote, full-time role lasting six months. This opportunity is ideal for ambitious young Africans seeking hands-on experience in digital financial inclusion, project coordination, payments ecosystems, and development-focused initiatives that are transforming financial systems across the continent. The application deadline is 29 May 2026. About AfricaNenda Foundation AfricaNenda Foundation is a leading organization focused on accelerating the growth of inclusive instant payment systems throughout Africa. The foundation works with governments, financial institutions, fintech companies, regulators, and development partners to improve access to safe, affordable, and interoperable digital payments. Its mission is centered on advancing financial inclusion by supporting payment infrastructure that enables individuals and businesses to send and receive money instantly and securely. AfricaNenda plays a major role in helping African countries build stronger digital economies while supporting economic empowerment, innovation, and cross-border financial integration. About the Program Support Internship The AfricaNenda Foundation Program Support Internship is designed to support the Projects Team in implementing initiatives related to inclusive payment systems and financial inclusion across Africa. The internship offers participants the opportunity to work in an international and impact-driven environment while gaining practical experience in programme coordination, project management support, stakeholder engagement, and operational support. This is a paid internship and will be conducted remotely, making it accessible to qualified candidates across different African countries. Internship Details Organization: AfricaNenda Foundation Position: Program Support Intern Internship Type: Paid Internship Work Mode: Remote Duration: 6 Months Employment Type: Full-Time Application Deadline: 29 May 2026 Why This Internship Matters Digital payment systems are becoming increasingly important for economic development, entrepreneurship, trade, and financial inclusion in Africa. Instant payment systems help individuals and businesses transact more efficiently, reduce dependence on cash, and improve access to financial services. Through this internship, participants will gain exposure to: Financial Inclusion Projects Understanding how payment systems support underserved populations Learning about inclusive financial ecosystems Digital Payments and Fintech Ecosystems Exposure to payment infrastructure initiatives Understanding instant payment systems in African markets International Development Work Collaborating with experts across multiple countries Supporting continent-wide development initiatives Remote Professional Experience Working in a multicultural and international remote environment Building communication and coordination skills Project and Programme Support Assisting project teams with coordination and implementation activities Supporting reporting and administrative processes Benefits of the AfricaNenda Internship Selected interns may benefit from: Paid internship experience Full-time remote work flexibility Exposure to Africa-wide financial inclusion projects Professional networking opportunities Experience with international development organizations Skills development in programme support and coordination Experience in digital finance and payment systems Opportunity to contribute to impactful projects across Africa Who Should Apply? This internship is suitable for young professionals and graduates interested in: Financial inclusion Fintech and digital payments Project management International development Economic development Innovation ecosystems Public policy Technology-driven development initiatives Candidates passionate about transforming Africa’s financial systems and supporting inclusive economic growth are especially encouraged to apply. Skills and Qualities That May Strengthen Applications Although detailed eligibility requirements were not fully listed in the announcement, strong candidates are likely to demonstrate: Excellent communication skills Organizational and coordination abilities Interest in digital finance and innovation Strong teamwork and collaboration skills Ability to work independently in remote settings Research and administrative support skills Interest in African development and financial inclusion Why Young Africans Should Consider This Opportunity Africa’s digital economy is growing rapidly, and payment systems are at the center of economic transformation. Internships like this provide early-career professionals with valuable exposure to the future of finance and technology on the continent. Working with AfricaNenda Foundation can help participants: Build a strong professional network Gain practical international experience Strengthen their CV for future opportunities Learn directly from experts in digital payments and financial inclusion Contribute to meaningful development initiatives impacting millions of Africans Application Deadline The deadline to apply for the AfricaNenda Foundation Program Support Internship is: 29 May 2026 Applicants are encouraged to apply early and prepare strong application materials highlighting their interest in financial inclusion, development, and digital transformation in Africa. Please send in your CV highlighting relevant skills and experiences, including three referees through the applications@africanenda.org email by 29th of May 2026. Female candidates are highly encouraged to apply. Final Thoughts The AfricaNenda Foundation Paid Program Support Internship 2026 is an excellent opportunity for young Africans seeking meaningful professional experience in financial inclusion and digital payments. With remote flexibility, paid support, and exposure to continental projects, this internship offers a strong platform for career growth in development, fintech, and innovation ecosystems. Young professionals passionate about Africa’s digital future should strongly consider applying before the deadline. For more information about this internship visit here .
